# 2026-05-07 — Q2 Portfolio Review

Quarterly internal review. Three companies on the docket: [ACME](../companies/acme-corp.md), [BetaWidgets](../companies/betawidgets-inc.md), GammaTech (private — no vault note yet). Notes are my own, not the official minutes.

## Attendees

- [Yuki Tanaka](../people/yuki-tanaka.md) (chair)
- [Dana Liu](../people/dana-liu.md)
- [Marcus Reyes](../people/marcus-reyes.md)
- [Sarah Chen](../people/sarah-chen.md) (me, notes)
- [Tariq Osei](../people/tariq-osei.md) (joined for the BW segment, 25 min)

## ACME positioning (40 min)

[Yuki](../people/yuki-tanaka.md) opened with the [Q2 IC framing](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md): we need to land a position by [2026-05-22](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#timeline). Three things to resolve:

1. Comparable set — see [decision trace](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#comparable-set). dana and i still disagree, target friday.
2. Aftermarket sub-segment treatment in the model — see [acme-corp.md#sub-segments](../companies/acme-corp.md#sub-segments-worth-distinguishing). dana skeptical it's not already priced in.
3. Tariff sensitivity in the bear case — [marcus](../people/marcus-reyes.md) thinks our bear is too soft.

> [!quote] dana, on the aftermarket point: *"i agree the segment is great. i don't agree that we know more about it than the market. why would street miss this?"*

fair pushback. my answer: street treats Widget Distribution as commodity, doesn't bother to disaggregate. but i don't have a clean way to prove that *they don't already know* — see [open questions](#open-questions) below.

## BetaWidgets (25 min, with Tariq)

[Tariq Osei](../people/tariq-osei.md) joined. Three things he shared, in his confidence order:

1. **Globex concentration is real and growing.** ~22-24% of BW revenue. confirmed by him via two procurement contacts at OEM competitors. (i later [confirmed independently](../daily-notes/2026-05-09.md) — 23.4%.)
2. **Globex renewal got worse for BW.** terms unspecified but tariq said "materially worse — they didn't like it." worth ~150-200bps gross margin headwind if it holds.
3. **Tariff resolution post-midterm.** consensus among his procurement contacts. less differentiated info than the first two.

[Dana](../people/dana-liu.md)'s pushback on #1 and #2: globex switching costs are high enough that the relationship is sticky even with worse terms. plausible — see [our disagreement](../daily-notes/2026-05-09.md#conversation-w-dana).

## GammaTech (10 min)

Brief. They're private, we have limited visibility. [Marcus](../people/marcus-reyes.md) thinks they may IPO 2027 which would give us a third comp. parking until then.

## Decisions made

- [ ] **comparable set** — owners: sarah + dana, deadline friday — [decision trace](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#comparable-set)
- [x] **add globex risk as named risk in IC memo** — yuki signed off, sarah to update [memo](../memos/acme-investment-memo.docx)
- [x] **proceed with [DCF framework](../methodology/dcf-framework.md) as-is for Q2** — no methodology changes mid-cycle
- [ ] **tariff bear case revisit** — marcus + sarah, by [2026-05-15](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#timeline)

## Open questions

- how does the street currently model ACME's aftermarket sub-segment? (talked about, no answer)
- is the BW globex relationship "sticky-but-deteriorating" really an investable thesis or just a slow leak? — see [betawidgets-inc.md#margin-trend](../companies/betawidgets-inc.md#margin-trend)
- successor risk on BW's CEO — does it matter for our thesis or is it BW's problem only?

## Action items for me

- [x] update [BW company note](../companies/betawidgets-inc.md) with confirmed globex %
- [ ] redo margin bridge for BW with corrected COGS — see [daily 2026-05-08](../daily-notes/2026-05-08.md#tomorrow)
- [ ] draft [comparable set memo](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#comparable-set) for friday
- [ ] update [memo base case](../memos/acme-investment-memo.docx#base-case) tariff assumptions (waiting on marcus)
